{{Quote|We have to vent the toxin from the harvesting machine. Once the shark is dead, we can get back to mining the Kolto.|Kono to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
'''Kono Nolan''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who worked for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==Biography==
During his work for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]], Nolan served as the chief [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] at the secret [[Hrakert Station]] on Manaan's [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] floor as of [[3956 BBY]]. When it was overrun by insane [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] he, along with [[Sami (Human)|Sami]], was one of the few survivors that were saved by the amnesiac [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] during his search for the [[Star Map]].

Despite his position as a scientist for the Republic, Nolan was amoral, against believing the ethical consequences of his work. He advocated using an experimental poison to kill the [[Progenitor]], and was furious when Revan, following Sami's advice, chose to destroy the facility instead.

==Behind the scenes==
If the player takes the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] path, he can have Revan poison the Progenitor and/or kill either or both of the scientists. The consequences of releasing the poison, however, are profound; Revan is then banned from returning to Manaan and the general price of [[medkit]]s throughout the game goes up; alternately, if the dark-side player has enough skill in Persuade, Revan can revisit the planet, using his ability to convince the Selkath to keep the matter quiet.
